subject: Accounting
title: Subsidiary Ledgers
description: A short lesson to help you apply Subsidiary Ledgers.

hook.text: A company has 500 customers who owe money. The general ledger shows one total. Who owes what?

buildup.text: Subsidiary ledgers break down a general ledger account into individual detail records.

discovery.text: The accounts receivable subsidiary ledger has a separate page for each customer's balance.

twist.text: The general ledger total must always match the sum of all subsidiary records—a built-in control.

climax.text: Without subsidiary ledgers, tracking who owes what and who's been paid would be impossible.

punchline.text: The total hides the details. Subsidiary ledgers reveal them.

quiz.question: What does a subsidiary ledger do?
options: Breaks down a general ledger account into individual details
